Whether you're a **recent graduate** , exploring a **career change** , or an **ESL/TEFL\-certified teacher** ready to take your skills overseas, this is the opportunity you’ve been looking for. **English 1** is now hiring **motivated, globally minded individuals** for **full\-time English teaching jobs in China** . No teaching experience? No problem. We provide **full training** , **ready\-made teaching materials** , and **visa sponsorship,** making this the perfect **graduate job** , **first job** , or **international career move** for anyone ready to start something new. **What you'll do** * Teach English to young learners aged 3–16 in a fun, interactive, and immersive classroom setting. * Deliver high\-energy lessons using English 1’s award\-winning curriculum and digital tools. * Provide constructive feedback and help students build confidence in their English skills. * Take part in school events, cultural activities, and student engagement programs. **What you’ll get** * Competitive salary \+ performance bonus after 6 months * 21 days paid leave (10 days paid leave \+ 11 days statutory holidays) * Flight allowance * International health insurance (co\-paid) * Airport pick\-up support * \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa \+ up to 2 weeks sponsored hotel stay * Comprehensive induction training, ongoing support, and fully prepared teaching materials * TEFL certificate \+ skill development \& career advancement * Distance learning courses (Level 6 Trinity College CertPT, DELTM) * English 1\-organized social and cultural activities \+ free Mandarin Chinese lessons *The hotel accommodation for the first two weeks will be deemed as a sponsorship upon successful completion of the first\-year contract. \*\*Sponsored legal Z work visa expenses will be reimbursed.* **Who we’re looking for** * A bachelor’s degree (in any subject) * A valid passport * A genuine interest in education, travel, and cultural exchange. * ESL certification (TEFL, TESOL, CELTA) is a plus\- we can sponsor * Clean background check. **Why work at English 1?** * Teachers are at the core of everything we do at English 1 * Over **30 years** of success in China with **200\+ schools** in **50\+ cities** . * Strong presence in **China** and **Indonesia** . * **Professional development** and career **advancement opportunities** . Whether you're graduating soon, pivoting into a new career, or seeking international experience, this opportunity offers a chance to teach, travel, and grow \#EntryLevel \#Graduates \#EnglishTeacher \#ESL \#TEFL \#TeachingJobs \#GraduateJobs \#hiring \#jobs \#entrylevel \#teachercns \#graduates \#esl \#tefl \#teach \#JobAlert \#NowHiring \#JobOpening \#CareerOpportunities \#JobSearch \#WorkWithUs \#LinkedInJobs \#JobSeeker \#OpenToWork \#CareerGoals \#JobHunt \#NewJob \#CollegeGraduates \#BachelorsDegree \#RecentGrad \#ClassOf2025 \#JobSearch \#CareerSuccess \#EntryLevelJobs \#HiringNow \#YoungProfessionals \#CareerGrowth \#Networking \#LinkedInNetworking \#FutureLeaders \#UniversityAlumni \#HigherEd \#AlumniNetwork \#CollegeSuccess

title: Hotel Management Graduate Programme 2026
description: NYX Hotels is giving laidback luxury: we are THE destination! Our NYX team (we call them City Lovers!) is a diverse bunch of vibrant people, who are on a mission to make NYX the first\-choice for guests looking for a high\-end stay with a unique, urban feel. NYX Hotels is a part of the Leonardo Hotels portfolio. **Please note: Successful applicants will be considered for opportunities across multiple Leonardo Hotels locations in the UK and Ireland.** Just about to graduate or graduated last year and wondering what’s next? With over 57 hotels in the UK \& Ireland and over 300 worldwide, and award\-winning L\&D offerings, we have exciting opportunities to not only take part in our graduate programme but also grow within an ever\-expanding company to achieve your career goals with the support of our L\&D programmes. **The Graduate Programme** * 18\-month Management Development Programme * The programme is a challenging mix of work experience, off the job learning and self\-learning * Exposed to as much of the hotel operation as possible through departmental rotation and relationship with Deputy General Manager or Operations Manager * Early focus on the Duty Manager role and responsibilities within the first 3\-6 months * Fixed term contract with the aim of securing a permanent management role by the end of the programme. **What we are looking for?** * A passion for genuine hospitality * A proactive, positive and resilient attitude * A degree in any of the following disciplines; Hotel/Leisure/Tourism or International Hospitality * Ideally, you will have previously worked in an operational role in Hotels, Leisure or Tourism * Flexibility on placement location to get the best out of your programme **What’s in it for you?** * 18\-month Management Development Programme * Extensive personal and professional development * Support of industry professionals with a dedicated mentor along with a programme sponsor. **What's next?** * Apply online here today! * Applications close July 2026 * We will be in touch by August 2026 * You will take part in a 2\-step process – 1 assessment centre and if successful, a final interview with the General Manager of your placement hotel * Opportunities to start with us from August/September with the graduate programme commencing November 2026 Some of the perks our Hotel Management Graduate Programme 2026 could enjoy include:* Special rates on Leonardo Hotel rooms across the UK \& Europe * Company\-wide recognition scheme: you could earn vouchers to spend on a wide range of high street shops * Talent referral scheme * Thank You Week: from ice cream trucks to yoga classes and lots in between! * Wellbeing Calendar * Ongoing job\-related training programmes with clear paths for progression We’re committed to creating a workplace where every individual—regardless of background, identity, or lived experience—is welcomed, valued, and respected for who they are. Belonging means embracing our differences and ensuring everyone feels seen, heard, and empowered. Why come join us as a Hotel Management Graduate Programme 2026? At NYX Hotels, we look after our City Lovers just as well as we look after our guests. Once you’re part of the NYX team, you’re part of the Leonardo Hotels community of over 55 hotels across the UK \& Ireland. Our regular Learning Bites, “Come Join Us Guest Care” training and Wellbeing webinars all help your progress with us. With support on tap, top\-notch practical training and clear progression you’ll be on track for a brilliant future in Hospitality!

title: Immigration Consultant
description: **Job Description** **Immigration Consultant (Cork)** **Contract** : Full\-time, permanent **Team** : Ireland Inbound **Office Location** : Cork, Ireland **The Role:** The Immigration Consultant will be responsible for managing the day\-to\-day casework of various Irish immigration matters, from case assessment to reviewing and filing applications, as well as providing advice to clients Immigration Case Work * Assess client objectives (typically multinational companies and high\-net\-worth individuals), providing guidance to achieve objectives and making compliance and regulatory risk assessments. * Conduct in\-depth assessment of immigration compliance pathways, including data collection, verification, and risk analysis for individual cases or larger projects. * Troubleshoot cases and establish strategy with the Manager, ensuring work is reviewed and signed off at key benchmark stages. * Prepare and draft employment permit applications, entry visas, and a range of immigration applications for Fragomen's multinational clients under Manager supervision. * Develop strategy for graduate Irish placements, complex EU Directive implementation, nationality and citizenship matters, and broader compliance work, providing advice to businesses, individuals, and families. Client Support * Provide training and immigration consultancy to clients, preparing presentations in advance to support process improvement and regulatory compliance. * Act as a key point of contact for advice on large\-scale business projects, global mobility regulatory and compliance requirements, and general project management. * Provide process overviews, regulatory updates, and contribute to strategy discussions with Global Mobility and HR teams; occasional onsite client visits may be required. * Liaise with government agencies such as DETE, ISD, and Irish Embassies abroad. * Determine parameters for internal data collection, interpret reports, and recommend solutions and proposals to clients. * Ensure all correspondence is clear, concise, and appropriate for the audience, maintaining client style. * Take ownership of client reporting. Team Support * Implement in\-house procedures to improve efficiency using Fragomen’s internal systems, and work with client accounts to ensure effective strategies, project management, and regulatory compliance. * Delegate work using software management tools. * Analyse case management data to identify best practices, address software pitfalls, and improve protocols and workflows. * Assist with office\-wide projects on efficiency, business development, and maintenance of the regulatory knowledge database. **What we are looking for:** The ideal person for this role will be a self\-starter with a background in a busy client facing role with excellent organisational skills. Also required: * Bachelor’s degree * Professional experience with immigration provider preferred * Client\-facing experience * Understanding of immigration compliance, regulatory and Right to Work Requirements * Strong interest in immigration as well as the desire to further career within this field * Knowledge of case and data management and risk analysis * Understanding of billing and billing procedures * Ability to work effectively with clients and cross\-functional teams * Strong ability to prioritize workload, manage competing deadlines, and consistently meet service level agreements * Excellent attention to detail and ability to multi\-task in a fast\-paced environment * Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to work effectively with clients, internal teams, and external stakeholders. **What to expect:** * Once you have submitted your application, our Talent Acquisition team will review your skills and experience. If these match the requirements of the position, a member of our team will contact you to schedule an initial phone call. * If shortlisted, you will be invited to participate in our interview process, which will consist of at least two stages, one of which will be conducted in\-person. Depending on the role, you may also be required to complete a written assessment. * After the interviews, we will carefully evaluate all candidates and make our final decision. You will be notified of the outcome and feedback will be provided. * At Fragomen, we are committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible workplace for all applicants and employees. If you require any specific accommodations or support during the interview process, please let us know, and we will consider what reasonable adjustments are possible to meet your needs **Who we are:** At Fragomen, we are more than a global immigration services provider – we are pioneers shaping the future of mobility. With a passion for navigating the complexities of immigration, we help individuals and businesses transcend borders, unlocking opportunities around the world. As a forward\-thinking, global business, we combine deep expertise with innovative technology to craft solutions that not only simplify the immigration process but also drive strategic success for our clients. Whether it is assisting multinational companies or helping people start new chapters in their lives, we are driven by the belief that global movement is the key to unlocking a world of possibilities. We are proud of our team spirit, and we like to reflect this in the way that we work and everything we do. Our Responsible Business Practices ("RBP") prioritize Fragomen's social responsibility through a cohesive strategy covering five key focus areas: Corporate Social Responsibility, Culture and Inclusion, Sustainability, Well\-Being and Pro Bono. Our RBP initiatives offer great opportunities for everyone at Fragomen to feel included, get involved and shape thoughtful initiatives and activities. For more information, please visit our website – https://www.fragomen.com/about/responsible\-business\-practices.html . *Fragomen is committed to promoting equal opportunities for all employees and applicants, regardless of race, ethnicity, heritage, gender, age, religion, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or intersex status.* *All offers and/or employment contracts are contingent upon the successful completion of the Firm’s pre\-employment screening process. This process may include verifying the candidate’s identity, confirming legal authorization to work in the offered position’s location, and conducting a comprehensive background check, where permitted by local regulations. We use limited AI‑assisted tools for administrative screening purposes only \- never for decision‑making. All hiring decisions are made by people. Applicants may have rights to information and explanations regarding the use of such tools, or request human review, as required by applicable regional laws.*

title: Investment Associate
description: Our client is one of the world’s largest and most successful investment firms with a 40\+ year track record of investment excellence and they are currently looking to hire and Investment Associate / Senior Associate to join their team. This position will sit on a team that is responsible for the analysis of their Asset\-Based Finance (ABF) portfolio. **Responsibilities will include:** * Monitoring of Asset Based Finance trading performance through detailed financial statements analysis \& dialogue with the company * Asset Based Finance, Asset\-Backed Securitisation, Asset\-Backed Securities (ABS), Asset\-Based Lending * Investment analysis * Financial modelling and sensitivity analysis * Preparation of quarterly reviews and presentation to Investment Committees **The key attributes required from successful candidates are:** * 4\-7 years relevant experience (i.e. investment banking, infrastructure, real estate, aircraft leasing, corporate finance, private equity, transaction services or restructuring) * Excellent academic background * ACA or CFA qualified not essential but prefered * Strong commercial awareness and a deep understanding of financial statements / financial modelling This role pays a market leading compensation package. If you are interested in discussing this role further, please feel free to contact Dylan Philpott at d.philpott@masonalexander.ie or \+353 1 522 2943\.

title: Java Developer
description: Java Developer Location: Dublin, Ireland (Hybrid) Type: Permanent, Full\-Time We are delighted to be partnering with a leading Irish wealth management firm to appoint an experienced Java Developer to join their growing Information Systems team. This is an excellent opportunity to work on business\-critical platforms within a highly regulated, technology\-driven financial services environment. **The Role** As a Java Developer, you will play a key role in the design, development, and delivery of scalable, secure, and high\-performance applications. You will collaborate closely with business stakeholders, vendors, and cross\-functional technology teams, contributing to both day\-to\-day delivery and longer\-term technical strategy. **Key Responsibilities** * Design, develop, and deliver high\-quality Java\-based applications in line with functional and non\-functional requirements. * Work collaboratively with business stakeholders, third\-party vendors, and internal technology teams to deliver robust solutions. * Contribute to the design and implementation of microservices\-based architectures. * Develop and maintain RESTful APIs and backend services. * Ensure solutions are secure, maintainable, and aligned with industry best practices. * Participate in code reviews, testing activities, and quality assurance processes. * Produce and maintain clear technical documentation, including design specifications and standards. * Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives within the development team and broader IT function. * Support and mentor junior developers, where appropriate. **Requirements** Education * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, IT, or a related discipline (or equivalent experience). **Experience** * 5\+ years’ professional experience in Java / J2EE development. * Strong experience within the Spring ecosystem (Spring Boot, Spring MVC; exposure to Spring Batch or Integration is beneficial). * Proven experience developing RESTful APIs. * Experience working with SQL and relational databases (e.g. Oracle, MySQL). * Familiarity with build tools such as Maven or Gradle. * Experience using Git for source control and modern IDEs (IntelliJ, VS Code). * Working knowledge of Linux environments. * Experience delivering solutions within a recognised SDLC (Agile or Waterfall). * Awareness of web application security principles and secure coding practices. * Exposure to web analytics and best\-practice integration is advantageous. **Why Apply?** Join a respected and established Irish wealth management organisation. Work on complex, enterprise\-scale systems in a regulated financial services environment. Long\-term, permanent role with strong investment in technology and innovation. Collaborative culture with opportunities for technical growth and progression. If this position is something of interest, please apply directly or reach out to Daniel O'Connell at doconnell@elevatepartners.ie

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor - Construction - Developer
description: **Junior Quantity Surveyor – Residential Developer** **Location:** Lucan, Dublin **Working Arrangement :** Office\-Based with Flexibility **Sector:** Residential Developer – Housing Projects **Type:** Full\-Time, Permanent **Open To:** Contractor, Developer or Consultancy Background **Salary:** 40K \- 50K Base \+ Pension **Role Overview** A well\-established residential developer delivering large\-scale housing schemes is seeking a Junior Quantity Surveyor to join its commercial team. This role offers the opportunity to work within a structured developer environment, supporting the delivery of high\-quality housing developments from early\-stage cost planning through to project completion. You will work closely with Senior QSs and the wider development and construction teams, gaining exposure to all aspects of developer\-led cost management and procurement. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in preparing cost plans, budgets, and financial appraisals for housing developments. * Support the procurement of subcontract packages in line with programme requirements. * Assist in preparing and reviewing valuations, variations, and cost reports. * Track project costs and support monthly financial reporting processes. * Liaise with site teams to gather progress and cost information. * Support tender processes and subcontractor engagement. * Assist in maintaining cost control systems and documentation. **Candidate Requirements** * Degree in Quantity Surveying or related discipline. * 1–3 years’ experience (including placements) in a QS role. * Strong interest in working within a developer\-led residential environment. * Good understanding of construction processes and cost management principles. * Strong organisational and communication skills. * Proficient in Microsoft Excel and general IT systems. * Ambitious and keen to develop within a structured team. This is a confidential opportunity, and full details will be shared only with genuinely interested and suitable candidates. To arrange a confidential chat or request more information, contact: shane@lumensearch.ie \- 083 207 7469

title: Junior Quantity Surveyor – Civic ITS (Ireland)
description: Civic ITS delivers the intelligent transport systems that keep Ireland’s roads safe, efficient, and moving. As our project portfolio grows, we’re looking for a **Junior Quantity Surveyor** who’s eager to learn, contribute, and build a long‑term career in a specialist engineering environment. This is an ideal role for someone with early QS experience — or a recent graduate — who wants hands‑on exposure to real infrastructure projects and strong mentorship from an experienced commercial team. **Role Overview** You’ll support the commercial and contractual delivery of ITS projects, working closely with senior QS staff, project managers, engineers, and suppliers. The role blends structured learning with real responsibility, giving you the foundation to grow into a fully autonomous QS within Civic. **Key Responsibilities** * Assist in preparing **cost estimates, budgets, and financial reports** * Support the development of **BOQs, tender documents, and procurement packages** * Help manage **subcontractor quotations, comparisons, and contract administration** * Track **project progress**, gathering data for valuations and variations * Contribute to **payment applications** and commercial documentation * Maintain accurate records and support compliance with **Public Works Contracts** * Work with project teams to monitor **costs, risks, and commercial performance** * Build strong working relationships with suppliers, contractors, and internal teams **About You** * Degree (or final‑year student) in **Quantity Surveying** or a Construction related field * 1\+ years’ experience in QS, commercial, or construction roles * Interest in infrastructure, electrical, civil, or technology‑based projects * Strong numerical, analytical, and organisational skills * Willingness to learn, ask questions, and take ownership of tasks * Good communication skills and a collaborative mindset * Full clean driving licence (or working toward one) **What Civic ITS Offers** * Competitive salary with clear progression pathways * Mentorship from experienced QS and commercial professionals * Exposure to nationally significant ITS and smart mobility projects * A supportive, unified team culture with strong governance * Training, CPD support, and opportunities to specialise Job Types: Full\-time, Permanent Pay: €35,000\.00\-€50,000\.00 per year Ability to commute/relocate: * Dublin, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (preferred) Application question(s): * Do you hold a Degree (or are final‑year student) in Quantity Surveying or a Construction related field? Experience: * QS, commercial, or construction: 1 year (preferred) Work Location: In person

title: Junior Sales Executive (Full Time)
company: Total Stainless and Engineering Supplies Limited
description: Total Stainless and Engineering Supplies Limited established in 2005 has built a strong reputation for competent stock holding and supply of stainless steel and general engineering products to all areas of the Irish engineering industry. We currently have an opening for a Junior Technical Sales Representative on a permanent full\-time basis with a passion and instinct for generating new business and seeking out business opportunities. Your role: Reporting directly to the Managing Director our internal sales role involves connecting with our current customer base to identify new sales opportunities, developing these opportunities using outbound calls, emails and follows ups, supporting our customers in understanding their needs while providing solutions and winning against our competitors. As an internal sales representative your duties are as follows: * Create, generate, and close new business. Eventually, working off one’s own initiative and independently without supervision. * Process orders internally. * Behave and operate in a fair, transparent, honest manner and value integrity in all work and behaviour. * Understand the customer’s needs and seek to find a solution. * Demonstrate strong commercial awareness and stay up to date with product information, industry developments and trends. * Communication effectively with customers and the wider operations team. * Develop and maintain customer relationships. * Responsible for customer follow ups. * Manage sales pipeline. * Generate leads, negotiate deals and upsell / cross sell products. * Explain product features and benefits to new and potential customers. * Maintain warehouse stock levels, pick orders and load / unload deliveries. Tend to the trade counter when required. Benefits: * Initial and continuous sales / stores training. * Pension scheme with employer contributions after 1 years’ service. * 21 days holidays per year. * Bonus Payments based on effort throughout the year. * Company car if progressing to field sales. * Gross Salary €35,000\. * This is an in\-house role; home working is not available, and field sales is not required. The ideal candidate will possess basic knowledge in the stainless\-steel industry and hold a full Irish driver’s licence / counterbalance forklift licence. We are seeking somebody agile, dynamic and energised who is looking to pick up a trade of sort and willing to dedicate the time and patience needed to learn what we do. If this role appeals to you and you believe you are a suitable candidate, please send a copy of your CV to holly@totalstainless.ie along with any other details you believe we may need. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: From €35,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company pension * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * DUBLIN 17, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Experience: * Sales Executive: 2 years (required) Language: * English (required) Licence/Certification: * B Driving Licence (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: Junior Sign Installation Engineer
description: About Prestige Signs Ltd Prestige Signs Ltd has built its reputation on delivering high\-quality outdoor and corporate signage for over 50 years, supporting businesses with branding solutions that are designed to last and installed to the highest standards. Prestige Signs Ltd are widely recognised as the leading manufacturer and supplier of outdoor signage and branding in Ireland. Our customers include many large national and multinational organisations, and a significant number have partnered with us for 10\+ years. This long\-term trust is earned through our focus on service, reliability, and workmanship, from project planning through to installation and ongoing maintenance. **We’re hiring**: Junior Installation Engineer (Outdoor \& Indoor Corporate Signage) To support continued growth, we are expanding our nationwide service and installation team. We now have an exciting opportunity for a Junior Installation Engineer to join our experienced team. While experience installing outdoor signage is desirable, it is not essential. We will provide full training and support. **Job Title:** Junior installation engineer – outdoor and indoor corporate signage **Location:** Nationwide although our base in Dublin 12 \- must be willing to travel. **Job Type:** Fulltime **Schedule**: Monday to Friday **Salary:** €30,000 \- €33,000 depending on experience. About the Role In this role, you will work closely with a senior installation engineer on the installation and maintenance of large corporate signage projects. This is a hands\-on position where you’ll learn practical, industry\-relevant skills and develop confidence working on real sites. Key responsibilities include: \- Assisting with the installation of outdoor and indoor corporate signage \- Supporting safe and efficient work on\-site under guidance from senior staff \- Learning maintenance processes to support long\-term performance of signage systems \- Working as part of a team to meet deadlines and quality standards \- Building professional communication skills when dealing with onsite customers What We’re Looking For The ideal candidate is: \-Motivated, reliable, and committed to doing work to a high standard \- A fast learner who takes instruction well and shows initiative \- Able to work effectively in a team environment \- Comfortable working outdoors and in different locations across the country \- Outgoing and customer\-focused, with the ability to communicate professionally on\-site \- Attentive to detail and proud of producing quality results Experience \- Outdoor signage installation experience is a plus but not required. \- Experience working with power tools is a distinct advantage. \- Full training will be provided for the successful candidate. \- Full Irish Driving licence an advantage, though not essential. Immediate Start Immediate start available for the successful candidate. Pay: €30,000\.00\-€33,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Company pension * On\-site parking Work Location: In person

title: Lecturer/AP in Computer Science, UCD Computer Sci, Perm or Temp
description: **Applications are invited for a permanent or temporary post of a Lecturer/Assistant Professor in Computer Science within UCD School of Computer Science.** The UCD School of Computer Science (ucd.ie/cs) is Ireland's leading computer science school in terms of teaching and research. We offer taught programmes at undergraduate and postgraduate levels, which attract the top students from Ireland and abroad. Our staff are research active and hold key leadership positions in internationally significant research centres such as the Insight Centre for Data Analytics (insight\-centre.org), CeADAR \- the Centre for Applied AI (ceadar.ie), ML Labs Centre for Research Training (ml\-labs.ie), and the UCD Centre for Cybersecurity and Cybercrime Investigation (www.ucd.ie/cci). School staff are actively involved in a number of EU projects in areas such as Digital Health, Cybersecurity, ML \& AI and HCI. The School is committed to excellence in teaching and research and to supporting the Information \& Communications Technology sector in Ireland. The new Assistant Professor/s will be expected to teach on the BSc and MSc programmes provided by the School, in particular to contribute to undergraduate and graduate teaching in cybersecurity and/or software engineering, and to contribute to the research activity of the School. Candidates with expertise in one or more of the following subject areas are particularly encouraged to apply: Cybersecurity, Software Engineering and Distributed Systems. **Important Note for applicants:** Please state clearly in the covering letter of your application any preferences you may have with regards to the available levels of appointment. If not otherwise specified, it will be assumed you wish your application to be considered for either level. **Candidates are also asked to submit a CV \& Cover Letter including sections covering:** \* Key Achievements in the Generation of Knowledge \* Key Achievements in the Development of Individuals and Collaborations \* Key Achievements Supporting Broader Society and the Economy \* Key Achievements Supporting the Research Community In addition, candidates should submit a max one\-page document describing 3 key research publications: the paper's significance, their specific role in the paper, the impact and whether it is open access. **95 Lecturer/Asst Professor Above the Bar (7943\) Salary Scale: €65,401 \- €103,564 Per Annum** Appointment will be made on scale and in accordance with the Department of Finance guidelines **Closing date: 12:00 noon (local Irish time) on 01 May 2026\.** Applications must be submitted by the closing date and time specified. Any applications which are still in progress at the closing time of 12:00 noon (Local Irish Time) on the specified closing date will be cancelled automatically by the system. UCD are unable to accept late applications. UCD do not require assistance from Recruitment Agencies. Any CVs submitted by Recruitment Agencies will be returned. **Note:** Hours of work for academic staff are those as prescribed under Public Service Agreements. For further information, please follow the link: https://www.ucd.ie/hr/t4media/Academic Contract.pdf Prior to application, further information (including application procedure) should be obtained from the Work at UCD website: https://www.ucd.ie/workatucd/jobs/ **Desired Skills and Experience** Communication and interpersonal skills, Ability to deliver high quality teaching, A track record of research achievement, Ability to work as part of a team, Fluency in English, Experience of mentoring or supervision

title: Legal Counsel – Multinational Tech Company
description: My client is looking for an experienced Legal Counsel to join their team in Dublin. They are a major Multinational company growing their team in Ireland at scale. You’ll provide legal advice to management on strategic initiatives, business operations, regulatory matters, and compliance in Ireland. Lead corporate legal activities and ensure timely, effective legal support across the business, including drafting, reviewing, and negotiating contracts. **Key Responsibilities** * Draft, review, and negotiate a wide range of commercial contracts and legal documents. * Advise on contractual, statutory, and regulatory obligations, ensuring ongoing compliance in customer engagements. * Conduct legal due diligence and research to support business decisions. * Provide legal counsel across corporate, commercial, and risk management matters. * Identify and mitigate legal and operational risks. * Interpret statutes, regulations, and contracts, and advise on resolving legal and business issues. * Partner with internal stakeholders to deliver clear, practical legal guidance. * Manage and support legal projects and, where required, supervise junior legal staff. * Report regularly to the Head of Legal on key matters and developments. **Skills \& Experience** * Qualified lawyer (LLB/LLM/JD or equivalent) with a current practising certificate. * 5–10 years’ experience, including at least 5 years in outsourcing, IT contracts, or licensing. * Strong drafting, negotiation, and analytical skills, with solid knowledge of relevant laws. * Effective communicator with the ability to work across all levels and cultures. * Strong organisational, project management, and prioritisation skills. * Collaborative, proactive, and able to operate independently with minimal support. * Experience managing or mentoring team members is an advantage. * Willingness to travel domestically and internationally as required. Salary dependent on candidate experience. Benefits: Annual Bonus Scheme. Contributory Pension. Private Medical Insurance. Life Assurance \& Long\-Term Disability. Employee Assistance Programme. 22 days annual leave \+ 10 public holidays. Continuous Learning \& Development. Access to extensive training \& certification resources. Lunch \& Learn sessions. Additional perks including company discounts, on\-site parking, and bike\-to\-work scheme **Based in Dublin, Ireland. Hybrid.** Candidates must be eligible to work in Ireland/EU. For more information, please contact **David Coyle** at **01 635 1748** or email **david@methodius.com**

title: Lending Business Analyst
description: NTT DATA strives to hire exceptional, innovative and passionate individuals who want to grow with us. If you want to be part of an inclusive, adaptable, and forward\-thinking organization, apply now. **Summary of Role:** * Bring a good understanding of the Lending and Credit domain having worked in similar capacity earlier * Interact with business users and technical team for understanding of functional requirements * Document business requirements and process flows using different tools like JIRA, Confluence, Blue Works, Lucid Chart, MS Visio etc * Analyse and design solutions with the support of Solution Architects * Provide feedback on test cases and support to Quality Assurance for testing * Support in client UAT **Responsibilities of the role:** * Engage with business users to define scope, understand business processes, problem statements and pain areas * Identify key stakeholders and construct stakeholder analysis i.e. how they are affected * Support the identification of risks and mitigation approaches * Participate in client requirement workshops and identify gaps together with high level proposals for their resolution (e.g. development workarounds or change in processes). Ensure any solutions proposed are realistic and within the agreed scope of the project. * Analyse client business requirements, liaise with clients / project teams and prepare business process documents like BRDs, User Stories, Discussion Logs, Workflows etc. Be involved actively in the process till the sign\-off is not obtained from the client. * Participate in Solution design with the Design and Development team to ensure requirements are duly addressed. Ensure Requirement Traceability Matrix is updated to facilitate tracking of requirements coverage. * Make appropriate recommendations that will positively impact operational effectiveness, reduce cost and provide a better customer experience * Conduct relevant research to increase understanding in support of developing comprehensive solutions * Participate in final User Acceptance Testing for all major developments in the domain * Assist in the production of various collaterals such as User Guide, SOP Documents, process flows etc **Experience** * A strong background in Lending domain having good understanding of end\-to\-end lending lifecycle in areas such as lead management, loan origination, loan management and servicing, collateral management and delinquency handling * Should have good understanding of gamut of retail products loans such as Personal Loans, Mortgages, Vehicle Loans, Credit Cards etc and corporate products such as Overdrafts, Working Capital, Bill Discounting, Trade Finance etc * Familiarity with leading banking applications like FinnOne Neo, Finacle, Flexcube, TCS BaNCS, T24 is a plus * Design thinking, ability to manage multiple stakeholder expectations, communicate in a clear non\-ambiguous manner, conduct requirements workshops, drive decision making * Experience in documenting business case, business process modelling, requirement elicitation through workshops * Manage projects from inception through design to delivery * An IT background with a solid grounding in technology * Experience of working in both Agile \& waterfall SDLCs * Communication, group dynamics, collaboration and continuous improvement are core – being best practice driven * Pro\-active, quick learner, ability to effectively work in multi\-cultural and multi\-national teams * Positive attitude and ability to engage with different stakeholders managing scope and expectations skilfully **About NTT DATA** NTT DATA is a $30 billion business and technology services leader, serving 75% of the Fortune Global 100\. We are committed to accelerating client success and positively impacting society through responsible innovation. We are one of the world's leading AI and digital infrastructure providers, with unmatched capabilities in enterprise\-scale AI, cloud, security, connectivity, data centers and application services. our consulting and Industry solutions help organizations and society move confidently and sustainably into the digital future. title: Loss Prevention Officer
description: TK Maxx At TJX Europe, every day brings new opportunities for growth, exploration, and achievement. You’ll be part of our vibrant team that embraces diversity, fosters collaboration, and prioritises your development. Whether you’re working in our Distribution Centers, Corporate Offices, or Retail Stores—TK Maxx \& Homesense, you’ll find abundant opportunities to learn, thrive, and make an impact. Come join our TJX team—a Fortune 100 company and the world’s leading off\-price retailer. **Job Description:** **Could you be one of our Loss Prevention Officers in TK Maxx Dundrum?** As the world's leading off\-price retailer of clothing and homeware, we're all about putting our customers first. With thousands of shops worldwide, we aim to provide a positive and memorable experience for all. Working as a **Loss Prevention Officer**, you'll help to deliver our goal of keeping our customers, associates and assets safe. Experience of being in a customer facing role, within a fast\-paced retail environment or security guard role is ideal but not essential. **Candidates are required to hold a valid Security Guard (Static) licence issued by the Private Security Authority.** **In this role:** You will be supporting a positive customer experience within our stores by being a visible, friendly, approachable presence at our entrance doors. You will be focused on deterring loss on the salesfloor whilst acting as an ambassador for the loss prevention function. You will work closely with the store team to maintain security of our product and together you will aim to deliver effective conflict resolution when required. You will need to demonstrate your ability to remain calm and professional under pressure. You will also be self\-motivated, resilient and driven to make a difference with a natural ability to communicate. **In return, you'll get an in\-depth programme of training when you start.** **Are we right for you?** We're all about helping you develop yourself. The skills you'll learn working within Loss Prevention will help you be at your best. Plus, you'll be surrounded by the support of our fun and caring team. Also as a TJX Europe associate, you'll enjoy such things as: Competitive Salary Uniform Provided Comprehensive package of benefits to support your mental, physical and financial wellbeing such as access to an employee assistance program, associate discount and service awards Join us, and you’ll be a part of our thriving, growing European business filled with opportunities for growth and recognition. We’re proud of our culture, we work hard, have lots of fun, and give back to many causes we care about. It will be a role you won't just like but love. As proud as we are of our past success, it’s our future that excites us most. We strive to provide opportunities for growth, recognition and a competitive salary and benefits package. Share our determination to think bolder and bigger, and be part of our future. We consider all applicants for employment without regard to age, disability, gender, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnerships, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief and/or sexual orientation.**Address:** Unit 3\.4**Location:** EUR TK Maxx IE Store 436 \- Dundrum

title: Mainframe Engineer
description: Are you a Softwar looking for your next big opportunity contracting in Ireland? Then I have the job for you! **About Your New Employer** * One of the biggest privately owned investment banks in the world. * Be part of one of the key teams for the business globally. * You will be focused on delivering cutting\-edge retirement application software. **About Your New Job as a Mainframe Engineer** * You will be a key individual contributor on an agile team delivering high\-quality mainframe solutions for financial services. * You will design, develop, and maintain software using mainframe technologies such as COBOL, DB2, CICS, and related tools. * You will participate in the full software development lifecycle, including architecture and application design, deployment, and automation. * Your role involves close collaboration with product owners, technical leads, and cross\-functional team members in an agile/SCRUM environment. * You will have the opportunity to guide, encourage, and motivate your fellow engineers, fostering a culture of engineering excellence. **What Skills You Need in this Job as a Mainframe Engineer** * Senior level experience with software design and development experience. * Essential expertise in Mainframe technologies (COBOL, DB2, CICS, COBOL\-stored procedures, NSP). * Experience developing and deploying batch and CICS applications. * Good working knowledge of mainframe tools (ENDEVOR, FILE\-AID, ABEND\-AID, CONTROL\-M). * Proven track record in product delivery within agile scrum development environments. * Strong analytical, communication, organisational skills, and ability to manage multiple tasks. * Financial services experience is a plus but not required. * Bonus knowledge: Java, web services (REST/SOAP), AWS, containerisation. **What’s on Offer in this job as a Mainframe Engineer** * Daily Rate up to €550/€600 * Hybrid working environment that is remote the majority of the month with one full week on\-site per month * Good likelihood of contract extensions **What’s Next** If you think this job is for you, apply now! Looking forward to talking to some great candidates!

title: Maintenance Engineer
description: **Electrical Maintenance Engineer (Shift\-Based \| 4\-Day Work Week)** **Be part of something being built, not just maintained.** This is a great opportunity to join high\-volume manufacturing operation entering an exciting phase of growth and investment. As part of this, a **new Electrical Maintenance Engineer role** has been created. If you’re someone who enjoys solving problems, improving systems, and being trusted to make an impact, this is a role where you won’t just “keep things running”… you’ll help define how they run in the future. **What You’ll Be Doing** * Diagnosing and resolving faults across production and site equipment * Supporting the reliability of automated machinery (e.g. conveyors, packaging systems, processing equipment) * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance and condition monitoring * Responding quickly to breakdowns to minimise downtime * Contributing to continuous improvement and reliability initiatives * Performing root cause analysis and implementing long\-term fixes * Maintaining accurate maintenance records and system updates * Working collaboratively with production and leadership teams * **Playing a key role in the installation, testing, and optimisation of new equipment** **What We’re Looking For** * Qualified in an electrical discipline (time\-served Craft Electrician or equivalent, ) * At least 2 years’ experience in a similar industrial or manufacturing environment * Strong fault\-finding and problem\-solving ability * Comfortable working hands\-on with both electrical and mechanical systems * A proactive mindset you don’t wait to be told, you take ownership * Flexible and team\-oriented approach to shift work * Good communication skills and attention to detail **What’s On Offer** * **€70,000 \+ DOE** * Pension Contribution 5% * 40\-hour week across a 4\-day shift pattern * Annual medical * Full PPE and your own professional toolbox provided **Working Hours \& Shift Pattern** * 4\-day working week (4 shifts over 7 days) * 4 x 10 hour shifts \-3 days off back to back * Start times rotate between: * 4:00am – 2:00pm * 6:00am – 4:00pm * 12:00pm – 10:00pm **Why Apply?** This isn’t a backfill role, you’ll be joining at a time of **real investment and change** . If you want to move beyond reactive maintenance and **get exposure to projects, installs, and improvements** , this is the right opportunity.

title: Maintenance Technician
description: Hollyfort, a private company specialising in public works for Government Bodies, including Healthcare and County Councils, is expanding! We're seeking multiple Maintenance Technicians to join our growing team to work on exciting projects throughout Dublin and Leinster. We’re looking for a practical, hands\-on Maintenance Technician who can handle a wide variety of household repair and improvement jobs. If you’re someone who takes pride in fixing things properly and leaving customers happy, this could be the role for you. **What You’ll Be Doing:** * Visiting homes, commercial properties, and public spaces to carry out maintenance and repairs. * Performing a wide range of tasks, including painting, patch repairs, tiling (preferred but not required), and general upkeep. * Undertaking and resolving maintenance problems to a basic level across all disciplines * Carrying out planned preventative maintenance to ensure properties remain in top condition. * Collaborating with tradespeople, general operatives, and subcontractors to complete projects efficiently. * Keeping accurate records of maintenance checks and documenting all work carried out. * Providing a friendly, reliable service that clients and customers can trust. * Proper and safely use of hand and power tools **What We’re Looking For:** * Versatile practical skills across domestic maintenance and repair work. * Experience with painting and decorating, patch repairs, and general upkeep. * Tiling, carpentry, or minor plumbing skills would be a bonus. * A hands\-on, problem\-solving approach and the ability to handle a variety of tasks. * Reliability, good communication, and pride in delivering quality work. * Valid Safe Pass and Manual Handling * Valid driving license **Why Join Hollyfort?** * Every day is different – no two projects or jobs are the same. * Excellent opportunity to grow and develop * We value skill, initiative, and the satisfaction of a job well done. * You’ll have the chance to use your versatility to make a real difference for our customers. Benefits: * Company Pension * Company mobile phone * Company events * Bike to Work and Tax Saver Scheme * Employee Assistance Program * Learning and Development * Uniform provided Job Type: Full\-time Benefits: * Company events * Employee assistance program * On\-site parking Experience: * Maintenance Technician: 1 year (preferred) Licence/Certification: * Safe Pass (preferred) * Manual Handling Certificate (preferred) * B Driving Licence (preferred) Work Location: On the road

title: Managing Consultant
description: *At Fruition Group Ireland we embrace our values of* ***Pride*** *,* ***Energy*** *and* ***Perseverance*** *and our colleagues embed them as the foundation of their work. Our colleagues are proud to work for Fruition Group, they are pro\-active with high energy to maintain our market\-leading status. With a diverse team we recognise that no two people are the same and we focus on results rather than unachievable KPIs and work collaboratively to achieve our goals whilst having fun along the way!* *We are a high growth organisation who work with a huge range of technology employers throughout the UK, Ireland, EU and USA and as a business we re\-invest profits to ensure we are leading in recruitment innovation in our marketplace and equipping our colleagues with the best environment, tools and training to help them succeed.* *Following the great success and growth for our team in Bray we are now opening a new office in Dublin City! You will continue to develop your network of candidates within the tech (perm and contract) space and continually keeping up to date with current trends. Where you get the chance to develop your mentoring and leadership skills, as you will begin to support junior colleagues with their day\-to\-day recruitment. ***Key Responsibilities*** * Development of accounts into key accounts * Searching for suitable Tech candidates who match our clients’ requirements * Headhunting and networking on sites such as LinkedIn / GitHub / Stack Overflow * Writing and posting job adverts * Telephone interviewing candidates to assess their suitability for specific roles * Arranging interviews for candidates and liaising with them during this time * Making job offers to suitable candidates and handling rejections * Taking references / collating relevant supporting documents * Building relationships with candidates to create a strong talent pipeline * Use social media to create a personal brand * Keep up to date with trends within the Tech industry * Building relationships with existing and future clients * Utilise internal tech such as SourceWhale, LinkedHelper and Paiger * Ensure all data is accurately recorded on CRM * Attending meeting with clients to explore potential opportunities * Negotiating fees and terms of engagement * Manage the team \& support junior colleagues with their day\-to\-day recruitment ***Key Skills and Capabilities*** * Outstanding communicational skills with the ability to adapt language to different audiences * Ability to build long—lasting professional relationships * Ability to handle difficult conversations * Excellent analytical and problem\-solving skills, attention to detail; well organised * Proactive and independent thinker * Excellent customer service skills * Strong presentation skills with the ability to convey key messages to diverse and wide audiences * Exceptional Stakeholder Engagement skills * Experience working to targets * Great negotiating skills * Excellent Experience using the telephone in a professional environment is desirable * Great understanding of technical recruitment * Experience within a sales environment is desirable ***Your Benefits to name a few:*** * **Competitive basic salary with uncapped earning potential** * **Lucrative commission – no thresholds!** * **Ongoing training with external learning providers** * **Clear and structured Career progression** * **Biannual winners trip to European destinations such as Marbella, Barcelona \& Amsterdam** * **Flexible working offer \- between the office and home** * **Quarterly lunch clubs for top achievers** * **Quarterly team socials** * **Friday early finish** * **Private healthcare** * **Pension Contribution** If you’re interested in joining Fruition as Managing Consultant, please get in touch for an initial confidential chat about our available opportunities, and we’ll happily share our story with you. For further information please email lois.gooding@fruitiongroup.com We are an equal opportunities employer and welcome applications from all suitably qualified persons regardless of their race, sex, disability, religion/belief, sexual orientation or age.

title: Night Support Worker
description: **About the porject:** Long\-Term Accommodation services provide support for single people who are homeless with mental health, addiction or personal care needs. Residents are mostly people who have been homeless for long periods of time and who do not have the capacity to live independently. Every resident has their own room and there are also large communal rooms and gardens. The project operates a case management system where staff provide support to residents through individualised support plans. Residents are also taught life skills to increase their ability to maintain independent or semi\-independent living. **About the role:** Contract Type – Full Time Permanent. Working Hours – a 5 week rolling roster of night shifts, 37\.5 hours a week, from Monday to Sunday availability. Salary \- \- €33,500 \-38,250 depending on experience \- Plus 12% allowance per nights Location – Dublin City Centre All our emergency services are provided on a low threshold basis with a harm reduction model and a full suite of services for clients. The aim of our emergency services is to support our residents to move on to and to be able to sustain long\-term accommodation. We have several measures in place to ensure a quality, wraparound support service is provided to enable people to move out of homelessness. As a Support Worker, you’ll help to meet client needs on a nightly basis; you will provide in\-the\-moment support and safety to clients. You will contribute to ensuring effective and consistent best\-practice standards are followed in the Dublin Simon Community emergency accommodation services. The Support Worker role is an opportunity to gain frontline experience and many support workers graduate on to project worker roles within the organisation. As well as our induction program, you will be provided with 10 training days throughout the year, as well as on\-the\-job coaching and mentoring, ensuring you have the support and training to succeed in this role. **Your responsibilities** * Carry out health and safety checks as per local policy and recording of same. ▪ Provide support to the clients at night, recording on support plans, updating PASS and nights bed list, encouraging sleep hygiene. * Provide care interventions, including daily living skills support, relapse prevention support. * Ensure all new clients receive a robust induction to the service and are aware of their rights and responsibilities. * Ensure building is secure at night and that all systems are working (alarms, CCTV, emergency response details) and adhering to the lone working policy. * Therapeutic and diversionary interventions, providing emotional and motivational support, promoting positive mental health attitudes, self\-esteem and self\-respect. * Support the manager in the implementation of the strategic and operational action objectives. Taking responsibility for developing and implementing strategic and operational goals. * Preparations of rooms for service user intake to ensure standards are maintained. ▪ Crisis and conflict management. * Ensure consistent handovers, diary, incident reports and follow\-ups, in accordance with organisation policies. * Accommodation management; ensuring routine maintenance is carried out, cleaning, security inspections and bedroom health and safety inspections occur, in line with operational procedures and standards. * Ensure neighbourhood policy is implemented and managed at all times. * Active interventions; harm reduction, overdose prevention. * Interpersonal skills development, dealing with relationships, family liaison and behavioural management issues. **Essential for the role:** * Level 6, 7 or 8 in a relevant field with 6 months experience in similar setting (including voluntary work and college placement) Or * Level 5 in relevant field with 12 months experience in similar setting (including voluntary work and college placement) * A Passion for helping people. * Flexibility to work in an agile environment. * Willingness to learn. * Experience in carrying out Health \& Safety inspections and audits. **Benefits to working with Dublin Simon Community:** * 25 days annual leave. * 5% matched contribution to your pension. * Paid Sick Leave Policy. * Paid Maternity Leave Policy. * Bike to Work Scheme. * Commuter Travel Tax Savers Ticket. * 1st\-week Comprehensive Training. * Progression Opportunities. ​Shortlisting will be in progress throughout the period that the vacancy is live, so you may be called for interview and/or the vacancy may be filled before the closing date. Job Types: Permanent, Full\-time Pay: €33,500\.00\-€38,250\.00 per year Application question(s): * In order to work in Ireland a non\-EEA National, unless they are exempted, must hold a valid Employment Permit. Do you require an Employment Permit to work in Ireland? * Garda Vetting applies to roles with access to children or vulnerable persons. We will check with An Garda Síochána for any criminal record or relevant information. Have you ever been, or are you currently, subject to criminal charges or investigations? Work Location: In person

title: Office Administrator (No Visa opportunities)
description: **About Our Client** We are partnering with the Irish operation of one of the world’s leading automotive brands based in Santry, County Dublin, to recruit a highly organised and proactive Office Administrator. This is an excellent opportunity for a motivated individual who enjoys taking ownership of administrative duties and thrives in a busy office environment. Our client values professionalism, reliability, and a positive attitude, and is looking for someone to become a key part of their team. **Role Overview** As the Office Administrator, you will be the central hub ensuring the smooth and efficient running of the office. This is a highly customer\-facing role where you will manage reception duties, warmly welcome visitors, and serve as the first point of contact for clients, suppliers, and other stakeholders. You will coordinate meetings and events, liaise with suppliers, and oversee health and safety compliance. The ideal candidate is a confident, organised individual who can multitask effectively while maintaining excellent communication skills and fluency in English to engage professionally with both internal teams and external partners. **Key Responsibilities** **Front\-of\-House \& Reception** * Manage all reception duties, including answering phone calls and welcoming visitors * Serve as the primary contact for building managers and external stakeholders * Coordinate visitor arrangements to ensure a professional and welcoming experience **Office \& Meeting Coordination** * Organise meetings for suppliers and retailers on behalf of the Managing Directors * Support office\-related projects and general administrative tasks * Schedule and manage internal meetings, training sessions, and office events **Office Operations \& Supplier Management** * Act as the main contact for office suppliers such as stationery, media, couriers, cleaning, security, and technical services * Monitor and maintain high standards of office cleanliness and workspace upkeep **Health, Safety \& Compliance** * Lead health and safety initiatives including fire prevention procedures * Manage insurance requirements for drivers, the building, and personnel **Candidate Profile** * Proven experience managing a busy office or department environment * Exceptional organisational skills with strong attention to detail * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, able to build positive working relationships * Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel, and PowerPoint * Full driving licence is desirable * Minimum qualification: Leaving Certificate; additional qualifications are advantageous * Fluent in English **Additional Information** * Work Location: On\-site in Santry, County Dublin * Commuting: Candidates must reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting * Work Authorisation: Must have legal authorisation to work in Ireland * Benefits: On\-site parking available If you are an organised, proactive Office Administrator looking to join a vibrant team and support a busy office environment, we encourage you to apply today. Job Type: Full\-time Pay: €34,967\.97 per year Benefits: * On\-site parking Ability to commute/relocate: * Santry, County Dublin: reliably commute or plan to relocate before starting work (required) Language: * fluent English (required) Work authorisation: * Ireland (required) Work Location: In person

title: PET CT Clinical Specialist
description: **Location:** On site / Dublin, Ireland **job type:** Permanent / Full\-time **Sector and subsector:** Medical \& Healthcare \| Allied Health Professionals **Salary:** Competitive Salary Alliance Medical currently have an exciting opportunity with an excellent remuneration package available for a PET\-CT Clinical Specialist to join our centre in Co Dublin! The PET\-CT Clinical Specialist will lead the PET\-CT unit, focusing on service delivery, quality assurance, and staff management. This role involves a combination of expert clinical duties, such as ensuring the correct protocols are followed and manage the day\-to\-day operations of the PET\-CT service, and leadership responsibilities, like training staff and supervising service delivery. **Key Responsibilities:** * In conjunction with the PET\-CT Clinical and Administrative staff, provide a safe \& efficient PET\-CT service, while adhering to all legislative regulations and company policies. * Accountable for the quality of the service provided, including customer service \& technical aspects. * Developing and implementing operational policies, protocols \& guidelines. * Maintain up to date knowledge of clinical, technical and radiographic developments in relation to the PET\-CT department and promote awareness of new developments. * Support the Unit Manager in the implementation of initiatives aimed at on\-going service development and improvement. * Promote \& maintain a safe environment for staff and patients. Assist in the development and implementation of risk management and health and safety strategies in association with the Quality Department. * Participate fully with all internal and external audits and Accreditation. * Quality Assurance: Investigate \& take appropriate action in accordance with Company policy in relation to complaints, accidents and incidents. * Ensure that all Company policies \& relevant up\-to\-date information is understood by staff and complied with. * Ensure all Company policies on infection control, IV policy and health and safety etc. are understood by staff and complied with. * Maintain a quality assurance programme in the PET/CT department. * Assist the Radiation Protection Officer to ensure that all principles and policies of radiation protection, are adhered to. * Be familiar with all legislation and directives regarding the safety standards for the protection of health workers and the general public against the dangers arising from Ionising Radiation. * Follow all Radiation Protection practices as determined by the Radiation Protection Committee, Radiation Protection Advisor and Radiation Protection Officer **Key Requirements:** * BSc (Hons) Diagnostic Radiography or equivalent * Active CORU Registration * IV Cannulation * Post\-Graduate Qualification in PET\-CT (desirable) but not mandatory. **Why choose Alliance Medical?** * Competitive Salary * Relocation Allowance *(t\&c’s)* * Comprehensive Induction \& training programme * Family Friendly Working Arrangements * 27 Annual Leave Days (including 2 discretionary days) * Full paid private Health Insurance * Paid Maternity Leave * Wedding Day Leave * Paid Sick Leave * Fertility Leave * Excellent Pension Scheme * Educational Assistance * Wellbeing Initiatives * Excellent overtime rates \#AMDI

Handbooks, Guidelines, and Information packs for staff and students undertaking school placement **Programmes** Oversee programme operations, including timetabling, module coordination, and scheduling of teaching activities Prepare and manage draft timetables (BA Education/Art and Design and Critical Cultures) and create programme schedules across trimesters ensuring these are shared with staff. Liaise with Programme staff within School of Education and other departments ensuring that all parties have access to relevant programme timetables and documentation. Identify and coordinate resource requirements, including staffing, facilities, equipment, and support systems Assist to the Head of School in preparing documentation for programme review/development/ as appropriate Liaise with Head of School to assist in the coordination of the annual recruitment process of staff and materials budget to support the delivery of the programme including collation of contracted hours Support quality assurance processes by organising student forums, compiling feedback, and contributing to annual programme reviews Support the programme team in identifying and addressing academic welfare concerns affecting students registered to the programme, escalating issues as appropriate. Ensure effective communication of programme materials, coordinate student induction, support assessment feedback systems, and assist in planning school placement timetables **General** Ensure compliance with policies and standards (e.g., garda vetting, health \& safety), contribute to meetings (including staff, programmes and teaching council meetings where relevant), and provide reports and updates on school placements and programme delivery Any other appropriate duties may be assigned by the Appropriate Officer from time to time. **Selection Criteria** Selection criteria outline the qualifications, skills, knowledge and/or experience that the successful candidate would need to demonstrate for successful discharge of the responsibilities of the post. Applications will be assessed on the basis of how well candidates satisfy these criteria. **Mandatory Criteria** (a) A minimum of 3 years relevant experience in an equivalent role PLUS relevant academic qualification (NFQ Level 6 or higher) OR (b) a minimum of 5 years relevant experience Strong administration skills and project management experience Have excellent communication, both written and verbal Have excellent interpersonal skills, with proven experience of building relationships with external and internal stakeholders High level of proficiency in IT, including strong excel skills and proficient in the use of project management software Have proven ability to work on your own initiative Proven ability to lead and manage complex projects involving multiple stakeholders Ability to drive innovation and meet deadlines and prioritise workload Experience in problem solving and decision making. title: Remote AI Quality Analyst - 62130
description: **About Turing:** Based in San Francisco, California, Turing is the world’s leading research accelerator for frontier AI labs and a trusted partner for global enterprises deploying advanced AI systems. Turing supports customers in two ways: first, by accelerating frontier research with high\-quality data, advanced training pipelines, plus top AI researchers who specialize in coding, reasoning, STEM, multilinguality, multimodality, and agents; and second, by applying that expertise to help enterprises transform AI from proof of concept into proprietary intelligence with systems that perform reliably, deliver measurable impact, and drive lasting results on the P\&L. **Role Overview:** As an AI Quality Analyst, you will evaluate a new personalization feature for Gemini. You will assess how well the model uses information from your past Gemini conversations, Gmail, Google Search, and YouTube activity to make responses more relevant and helpful. This role requires a unique blend of creativity and analytical rigor. You will actively design prompts from the perspective of your own personal experiences. You will then use your analytical skills to assess the quality of the model's personalized responses, evaluating dimensions like Grounding, Integration, and Helpfulness. **Key Qualifications:** * English Proficiency: Ability to read and write in English with a high degree of comp, as English is the focus language for this project. * Personal Account Usage: Willingness to use your primary personal Google account (not a testing account) and enable personal data sources for a genuine assessment. * Schedule Flexibility: Full\-time availability in your local time zone is required. We are staffing a global, 24\-hour operations team. * Exceptional Analytical Thinking: Demonstrate ability to evaluate nuanced and ambiguous AI responses, specifically assessing personalization quality. * Creative Prompt Engineering: Experience in designing creative, multi\-turn starting prompts based on personal context to thoroughly test the model's capabilities. * Strong Evaluation Acumen: Understanding of personalization concepts, including the ability to identify incorrect personalization, poor inferences, and forced connections. * Meticulous Attention to Detail: The ability to review Side\-by\-Side (SxS) model responses and spot subtle differences in naturalness and overnarrating. * Excellent Written Communication: Superior ability to write clear, concise, and structured rationales for model rankings, explicitly referencing specific turn numbers. * Feedback: Ability to provide constructive feedback and detailed annotations. * Communication: Excellent communication and collaboration skills. * Independence: Self\-motivated and able to work independently in a remote setting. * Technical Setup: Desktop/Laptop set up with a good internet connection. **Description:** * In this role, you will be part of a dynamic team focused on evaluating the quality of personalized AI interactions. **Your day\-to\-day work will involve:** * Designing and executing multi\-turn conversational prompts (typically 1\-5 turns) that require the AI to utilize your personal information and experiences. * Evaluating model responses based on your intent from the starting prompt, checking if the personalization was appropriately applied. * Analyzing responses for Grounding issues, ensuring claims about you are supported by evidence and not flawed inferences or hallucinations. * Assessing Integration quality to ensure personal data is woven naturally into the response without robotic "overnarrating". * Rigorously evaluating and stack\-ranking two model responses side\-by\-side (SxS) to determine which is overall more helpful, easy to use, and enjoyable. * Writing clear, defensible rationales for your comparisons, explicitly referencing where issues or positive aspects occurred in the conversation. * Extracting and verifying "Debug Info" from the model to confirm that chat summaries and data sources were properly utilized. * Maintaining strict data hygiene by deleting evaluation conversations to prevent them from polluting your future chat history. **Education \& Experience:** * BS/BA degree or equivalent experience in a relevant field (e.g., Policy, Law, Ethics, Linguistics, Journalism, Computer Science, or a related analytical field). * Experience in data annotation, AI quality evaluation, content moderation, or a related role is strongly preferred. **Offer Details:** * Commitments Required: at least 4 hours per day and minimum 30 hours per week with 4 hours of overlap with PST. (We have 2 options of time commitment: 30 hrs/week or 40 hrs/week) * Engagement type: Contractor * Engagement Length: 3 months **After applying, you will receive an email with a login link. Please use that link to access the portal and complete your profile.** Know amazing talent? Refer them at turing.com/referrals, and earn money from your network.
